title: Put nginx in front of your Node app

series.requires_description: Node app must be running on port 3000 and managed by pm2

problem: Your Node app is running on port 3000 but port 3000 should never be exposed directly to the internet. Configure nginx to sit in front of it — all requests come in on port 80 and nginx forwards them silently to port 3000. This is how every production app is served.

setup.description: Verify both nginx and your Node app are running before starting.
setup.note: All three must succeed. If curl on port 3000 fails go back and complete scenario 02 first.

hints: The nginx config on Amazon Linux lives in /etc/nginx/nginx.conf — find the location / block inside the server block
Replace everything inside location / with proxy_pass http://localhost:3000 and two proxy_set_header lines to forward the real client IP
Always run nginx -t before reloading — it validates your config and tells you exactly what is wrong if you made a syntax mistake
solution:
1. Open nginx config:
   sudo nano /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
   # Main config file on Amazon Linux.
2. Find the location block:
   location / { ... }
   # It is inside the server { } block.
3. Replace the block with:
   location / {
       proxy_pass http://localhost:3000;
       proxy_set_header Host $host;
       proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
   }
   # proxy_pass silently forwards all traffic to your Node app on port 3000.
4. Test config:
   sudo nginx -t
   # Never skip this — it catches syntax errors before reload.
5. Reload nginx:
   sudo systemctl reload nginx
   # Applies changes without dropping existing connections.
6. Test locally:
   curl http://localhost
   # Port 80 should now return your Node app response.
7. Test from outside:
   curl http://YOUR_EC2_IP
   # Run this from your local machine or browser.
8. Confirm port 3000 is private:
   curl http://YOUR_EC2_IP:3000
   # This should fail or timeout — port 3000 must not be publicly exposed.
